plan: Create `src/data/validateCardDeck.ts` with a `validateCardDeck(cards: Card[]): Card[]` function that: (a) rejects duplicate card numbers by collecting them and throwing a descriptive error, (b) rejects unknown tier values (reinforcing row-level checks at dataset level), (c) rejects unknown category values (reinforcing row-level checks at dataset level), (d) validates pairsWith references point to existing card numbers in the dataset
Create `src/data/normalizeCardDeck.ts` with a `normalizeCardDeck(cards: Card[]): Card[]` function that sorts cards by cardNumber in ascending order
Update `src/data/loadCardDeck.ts` to pipe parsed cards through `validateCardDeck()` then `normalizeCardDeck()` before exporting `cardDeck`

Create `tests/unit/validate-card-deck.test.ts` with tests: duplicate card numbers throw error, unknown tier values throw error, unknown category values throw error, invalid pairsWith references throw error, valid dataset passes without error
Create `tests/unit/normalize-card-deck.test.ts` with tests: cards are sorted by cardNumber ascending, already-sorted input remains unchanged, single-card input works, empty array works
Add integration test in existing `parse-card-csv.test.ts` (or new file) confirming the real 57-card CSV passes validation and comes out sorted
Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` to verify all checks pass
testing_recommendations: Unit test: duplicate card numbers array (e.g. two cards with cardNumber 5) throws descriptive error
Unit test: card with tier 'Unknown' is rejected with clear message
Unit test: card with category 'Nonexistent' is rejected with clear message
Unit test: card with pairsWith referencing non-existent card number throws error
Unit test: valid minimal dataset passes validation without throwing
Unit test: unsorted cards [3,1,2] are returned as [1,2,3] after normalization
Integration test: real card_deck.csv (57 cards) passes full validate+normalize pipeline
Integration test: exported cardDeck from loadCardDeck is sorted by cardNumber
risks: Row-level tier/category validation already exists in parseCardCsv.ts — dataset-level checks must complement, not duplicate, to avoid confusing double-error messages
Throwing errors at module load time (in loadCardDeck.ts) could crash the app on startup if CSV data is invalid — this is acceptable for a static build but should have clear error messages
pairsWith validation (checking references exist) is not explicitly in acceptance criteria but is strongly implied by 'safe and predictable content' — scope may need confirmation
